targetArchitecturalOutcome: The same root combination version must always resolve the same exact versioned dependency graph and produce the same bundle, regardless of what newer artifact versions are later added to the source catalog.

findings.title: CatalogPublisher validates the full graph before retired root combinations are filtered
findings.currentBehavior: ValidateSchemas, CatalogGraphValidator.Validate, CatalogStamper.StampAsPublished, and PublishReadinessValidator.Validate all run on the full unfiltered snapshot/stamped snapshot (including any retired combinations). eligibleCombinations (the retirement filter) is computed afterward, at line 74-77.
findings.risk: A legitimate new full-catalog release can be blocked entirely by a combination that was correctly retired (along with its now-invalid dependencies), even though that combination would have been excluded from the release anyway.
affectedReleases: none observed yet (retirements.json has never existed); latent in every release built by CatalogPublisher, 1.0.0 through 0.5.0-pilot
affectedActiveRootCombinations: any -- publisher-pipeline-wide, not specific to TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE
findings.proposedCorrection: Filter to non-retired combinations before running CatalogGraphValidator.Validate and PublishReadinessValidator.Validate, or have TemplateCombinationValidator skip a combination entirely once it is itself retired.
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: none -- pure ordering/logic change, no catalog artifact needs a new version

findings.title: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1, v2, v3 are all active sibling bundles in new full-catalog releases
findings.currentBehavior: 0.5.0-pilot's manifest lists 3 PUBLISHED_TEMPLATE_BUNDLE entries for TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE (v1 hash b6420adb..., v2 hash d0f23f4f..., v3 hash 5cf81183...). artifacts/appsel-plan-catalog/retirements.json does not exist on disk (confirmed by direct file check) -- nothing has ever been retired.
findings.risk: Every new full-catalog release grows an ever-larger set of sibling bundles for the same logical combination with no system-level notion of which is the active/default version.
findings.proposedCorrection: Introduce an explicit current/default version concept for a combination key (authoring-time pointer, or formal retirement of superseded versions).
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: none -- would be a ledger/policy addition, not a content/version change to any existing artifact

findings.title: Domain reports conflate three different blocker scopes into one misleading number
findings.currentBehavior: Line 97 reports 'PLACEHOLDER_UNCONFIRMED (blocking): 36' (the flat total across all PilotDomainContentAudit.Entries, spanning both historical-only and active-reachable versions). Line 106 reuses the identical number '36' while describing it as scoped to 'the dependency closure'. Measured directly in Task 3: activeRootClosureBlockerCount for v3 = 11; eligibleReleaseUnionBlockerCount = 12. Neither equals 36.
findings.risk: A reader or automated gate relying on '36' as the active-closure or eligible-release blocker count would be off by more than 3x.
affectedReleases: none directly -- reporting/documentation issue; PublishReadinessValidator itself correctly scopes to bundleArtifactTuples
affectedActiveRootCombinations: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE (all versions, undifferentiated by the flat figure)
findings.proposedCorrection: Report totalCatalogPlaceholderCount, eligibleReleaseUnionBlockerCount, and activeRootClosureBlockerCount as three explicitly labeled, separate numbers.

findings.title: Stale wording claiming combination v1 points to TEN_K_MASTER v2
findings.currentBehavior: Doc states 'catalog/combinations/ten-k-4d-intermediate.v1.json now points its masterTemplate reference at v2.' Actual file content (verified): masterTemplate = {key: TEN_K_MASTER, version: 1}. Wording predates COMB-IMMUT-001's restoration of v1 to its original (v1-referencing) content and was never updated afterward.
findings.risk: A reader trusting this sentence would believe combination v1 is version-inconsistent with its own immutable historical hash, re-triggering the confusion COMB-IMMUT-001 resolved.
affectedReleases: none -- documentation only; actual file content has been correct since the 0.4.0-pilot correction
findings.proposedCorrection: Update the sentence to state v1 was left unchanged (still TEN_K_MASTER v1) and v2 was the new artifact created to reference TEN_K_MASTER v2.

findings.title: WorkoutProgressionDefinition + FindWorkout's highest-non-retired-version resolution causes the same combination version to resolve a different bundle over time
findings.classOrMethod: WorkoutProgressionStageDefinition.WorkoutCandidateKeys (IReadOnlyList<string>); CatalogSourceSnapshot.FindWorkout(string key, IRetirementLedger?) lines 42-49; CatalogBundleAssembler.Assemble line 62
findings.currentBehavior: FindWorkout resolves Workouts.Where(key match && not retired).OrderByDescending(Version).FirstOrDefault() -- highest non-retired version, computed fresh at every assembly. LIVE PROOF: assembling TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1, v2, and v3 today all produce the identical effective-workout set (EASY_STANDARD v2, FARTLEK v2, GOAL_PACE_TEN_K v1, THRESHOLD_TEMPO v2) -- including v1, whose ORIGINAL 1.0.0-published bundle referenced workout v1s.
findings.risk: Directly violates the target architectural outcome. Any future new workout version silently changes the resolved bundle for every existing already-published combination version, with no version bump, audit trail entry, or cross-release-hash violation for the combination itself (only its derived bundle drifts).
affectedReleases: latent since CatalogBundleAssembler was written; observable starting 0.5.0-pilot (first release after WORKOUT_DEFINITION v2 artifacts existed); affects every future release
findings.proposedCorrection: Change WorkoutCandidateKeys from IReadOnlyList<string> to a list of versioned references so a stage pins an exact workout artifact.
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: TEN_K_WORKOUT_PROGRESSION_V1 already published/immutable since 1.0.0 -> requires v2. TEN_K_MASTER references WorkoutProgression via VersionedCatalogReference -> requires v3 (to point at progression v2). TemplateCombinationDefinition requires a new version (v4, since v1/v2/v3 already published) referencing TEN_K_MASTER v3. Same chain as Finding 9.

findings.title: LevelModifierDefinition also references eligible workouts by unversioned key
findings.currentBehavior: EligibleWorkoutKeys is a plain IReadOnlySet<string> -- no version pinning. CatalogBundleAssembler intersects progression candidate keys with EligibleWorkoutKeys.Contains (key-only membership) before resolving via the same version-drift-prone FindWorkout. LevelModifierDefinition's OTHER reference, ProgressionModifier, IS a fully-versioned VersionedCatalogReference -- only EligibleWorkoutKeys is key-only.
findings.risk: Same class of risk as Finding 5 -- participates directly in determining which concrete WorkoutDefinition artifacts end up in a bundle.
affectedReleases: same as Finding 5 -- latent since authored; observable since 0.5.0-pilot
affectedActiveRootCombinations: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1, v2, v3 (all use INTERMEDIATE_MODIFIER v1)
findings.proposedCorrection: Same shape as Finding 5 -- change EligibleWorkoutKeys to a versioned-reference set.
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: INTERMEDIATE_MODIFIER already published/immutable since 1.0.0 -> requires v2. Any combination adopting the fix needs a new version referencing INTERMEDIATE_MODIFIER v2 (same new combination version implicated by Finding 5, e.g. v4).

findings.title: TEN_K_MASTER v2 and active combination v3 select inconsistent RulePack versions through separate, uncoordinated fields
findings.currentBehavior: TEN_K_MASTER v2's $.requiredRules[0] = RULE_PACK/APPSEL_RACE_PLAN_V1/v1. TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v3's $.rulePack = RULE_PACK/APPSEL_RACE_PLAN_V1/v2. PlanTemplateValidator only checks the RequiredRules reference EXISTS, never cross-checked against any combination's RulePack field. CatalogBundleAssembler resolves the bundled rule pack exclusively from combination.RulePack -- RequiredRules is never read by bundle assembly at all.
findings.risk: RequiredRules is effectively dead/decorative data that can silently drift arbitrarily far from what a combination actually uses, with no validation error.
affectedReleases: TEN_K_MASTER v2 published since 0.3.0-pilot (declaring RequiredRules=v1 the whole time); inconsistency became concrete once combination v3 (RulePack v2) published in 0.5.0-pilot
findings.proposedCorrection: Establish a single owner for which RulePack a template/combination pairing requires -- either cross-validate RequiredRules against combination.RulePack, or deprecate RequiredRules for rule-pack purposes since bundle assembly already ignores it.
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: If reconciling by updating RequiredRules: TEN_K_MASTER already published/immutable at v2 -> requires v3; TemplateCombinationDefinition requires a new version referencing it. If deprecating RequiredRules: contract/schema-level change with its own migration path.

findings.title: WorkoutProgressionValidator selects the first runtime registry globally rather than the one pinned by the active RulePack
findings.currentBehavior: This single registry is used for every Requires condition check regardless of which RulePack any combination using this progression has pinned. Currently exactly one RuntimeConditionValueRegistryDefinition exists in the whole catalog, so FirstOrDefault() is correct today purely by coincidence of cardinality.
findings.risk: The moment a second registry is added, this validator picks an arbitrary one with no relationship to which registry the progression's actual consumers require, silently producing wrong validation results.
affectedReleases: latent in every release, 1.0.0 through 0.5.0-pilot; currently invisible because only one registry has ever existed
affectedActiveRootCombinations: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1, v2, v3 (all resolve TEN_K_WORKOUT_PROGRESSION_V1 v1, the progression this validator checks)
findings.proposedCorrection: Thread the actual governing registry into WorkoutProgressionValidator.Validate (resolved by the caller from the relevant combination's RulePack) instead of querying the snapshot globally.
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: none -- validator logic change only, no catalog artifact version change required

findings.title: LONG_RUN_STANDARD is absent from the active bundle despite an eligible LONG_RUN slot and level-modifier eligibility
findings.currentBehavior: RUN_LAYOUT_4D has a LongRun slot; INTERMEDIATE_MODIFIER.EligibleWorkoutKeys includes LONG_RUN_STANDARD. But LONG_RUN_STANDARD never appears in any phase/stage's workoutCandidateKeys in TEN_K_WORKOUT_PROGRESSION_V1 (confirmed via direct grep across the whole file: only EASY_STANDARD, FARTLEK, THRESHOLD_TEMPO, GOAL_PACE_TEN_K ever appear). Effective workouts = progression candidates INTERSECT levelModifier eligible keys -- LONG_RUN_STANDARD is excluded because it's never a candidate. Confirmed live in 0.5.0-pilot's v3 bundle: workouts = [EASY_STANDARD, FARTLEK, GOAL_PACE_TEN_K, THRESHOLD_TEMPO], no LONG_RUN_STANDARD. Identical for v1 and v2's rebuilt bundles today.
findings.risk: Genuine domain-content gap: no mechanism to ever schedule a long run despite the layout reserving a slot for one and the level modifier explicitly allowing it.
affectedReleases: 1.0.0 through 0.5.0-pilot -- TEN_K_WORKOUT_PROGRESSION_V1's content hash (a4856b47...) has been stable and unchanged across every release; this gap has existed since the first release
affectedActiveRootCombinations: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1, v2, v3 -- all three, identically
findings.proposedCorrection: Add LONG_RUN_STANDARD as a workoutCandidateKey to an appropriate stage (most naturally a FOUNDATION/BUILD long-run-building stage). Requires product/coaching judgment on phase(s)/stage(s)/exposure counts -- not decided by this read-only assessment.
findings.expectedVersionCascadeIfCorrected: TEN_K_WORKOUT_PROGRESSION_V1 already published/immutable since 1.0.0 -> requires v2. Cascades identically to Finding 5: TEN_K_MASTER new version (v3) -> new TemplateCombinationDefinition version (v4). Findings 5/6/9 share the same parent chain and would likely be delivered together.

findings.title: docs/README.md is incomplete relative to the governance rules current audits assume
findings.currentBehavior: Defines only: (1) a 6-level canonical-source precedence hierarchy for domain-content conflicts, (2) that docs/canonical/ is the approved canonical directory. Does not define: artifact immutability rules, retirement-ledger semantics, release-channel policy, cross-release hash-consistency invariant, the ContentDecisionStatus four-tier classification scheme, or what active root combination means when multiple non-retired versions coexist -- yet all of these are used pervasively and load-bearingly across existing audits.
findings.risk: Every one of these concepts is currently governed only by convention in individual audit reports and prior task instructions, not by a durable, discoverable governance document.
affectedReleases: none directly -- documentation-completeness issue, not a release-content defect
findings.proposedCorrection: Extend docs/README.md with dedicated sections for artifact immutability, retirement-ledger governance, release-channel policy, cross-release hash-consistency, and a formal ContentDecisionStatus definition, each citing the audit report where the rule was first established.
